返回日报
Hacker News #10

小米机器人1

Xiaomi-Robotics-1

AI 作者 ilreb 热度 498 回复 318 2026/07/20 12:45
打开原帖 打开外部链接 JSON

摘要

  • Breaking the data barrier. Scaling robot policy models with embodiment-free pre-training.Foundation models in language and vision keep moving the frontier by riding empirical scaling laws: capability tracks data, parameters, and compute. Robotics has missed out. Large-scale, high-quality data is hard to come by, and that scarcity, more than anything else, has capped how far policy models could scale. What robots can do under genuinely large-scale training remained largely an open question. We take a step toward answering it. Xiaomi-Robotics-1 combines large-scale embodiment-free (UMI) pre-training with a modest amount of real-robot data in a post-training stage. We study how the model behaves as it scales.DataEverything Xiaomi-Robotics-1 can do starts from data. For pre-training, we use 100,000 hours of embodiment-free (UMI) trajectories spanning more than 1,700 scenarios (household, commercial premises, industrial sites, and outdoor spaces), covering a diverse range of tasks. We develop a scalable auto-labeling pipeline that first divides trajectories into fixed-length segments and then annotates each segment with language descriptions of scene state transitions.For post-training, we leverage cross-embodiment datasets containing in-house robot data, filtered open-sourced robot data, and a set of high-quality UMI data. For the in-house data, we collected over 7,200 hours of real-robot data in real homes, covering tasks like tidying a sofa, sorting a shoe cabinet, and putting away kitchenware. The UMI data are manually annotated with temporal segments and instruction prompts, which differ from the auto-labeled state-transition descriptions used in the pre-training data.MethodFollowing the training paradigm of LLMs, the training of Xiaomi-Robotics-1 consists of two stages: pre-training and post-training. The first stage learns general representations for action generation from large-scale UMI data, while the post-training stage aligns the model with real robot embodiments and instruction-following capabilities.Pre-training is about breadth: exposing the model to as much of the real world as possible. We use the embodiment-free UMI data described above, which spans a broad range of environments and tasks. At this scale, manual labeling is infeasible. Thus, we built an automatic annotation pipeline powered by a strong vision-language model. Long videos are split into fixed-length clips, and the VLM describes the state transition of grippers and interacting objects within each clip. The result is a large-scale corpus of real-world manipulation trajectories, each annotated with precise language descriptions. These allow the model to learn action generation that drives the scene toward the state transitions described by the language.An encouraging finding is that pre-training shows a clean scaling behavior: as data and model size grow, validation action error steadily decreases.Post-training aims to align the strong action-generation capabilities acquired from pre-training with real robot embodiments and natural-language instruction following along two axes. Embodiment alignment uses high-quality cross-embodiment real-robot data to map the general action-generation ability onto actual robots. Instruction alignment shifts the model from "generating actions given a description of scene state transitions" to "understanding a natural-language instruction and executing it directly."After post-training, Xiaomi-Robotics-1 can be used out-of-the-box to perform a wide range of mobile manipulation tasks in the real world. We evaluate the post-trained model in unseen environments with unseen object instances to understand whether the scaling behaviors from pre-training can transfer to real-robot performance after post-training.The answer is yes. As we increase the amount of pre-training data and model size, real-robot success rate rises steadily and predictably. That is, a stronger pre-trained model yields better real-robot performance. The scaling gains show no signs of saturation: the real-robot success rate after post-training keeps improving as the model consumes more data or scales up during pre-training.ApplicationsAfter post-training, Xiaomi-Robotics-1 can serve as a strong robot foundation model for downstream applications. We put Xiaomi-Robotics-1 to use in two complementary downstream settings. Efficient adaptation to new tasks specializes the model to brand-new, highly complex real-robot tasks from a few hours of data per task. Simulation benchmarks probe its capabilities in mainstream suites that emphasize generalization.Xiaomi-Robotics-1 can learn new tasks with high data efficiency. The model picks up tasks like phone packing, printer refilling, laundry loading, and box packing from just a few hours of real-robot demonstrations per task. With an average of under 10 hours of demonstrations per task, it already reaches a 75% overall success rate, nearly doubling the π0.5 baseline (40%) at the same budget; raising the budget to an average of under 40 hours lifts overall success to 85%.Task<10 h/task on average<40 h/task on averageXR-1oursπ0.5XR-1oursπ0.5Phone Packing70308040Printer Refilling70206020Laundry Loading804010050Box Packing8070100100Overall75408553Evaluation on efficient learning of new tasks. Each cell shows success rate (%), higher is better. XR-1 = Xiaomi-Robotics-1.We evaluate Xiaomi-Robotics-1 on four mainstream simulation benchmarks. It achieves state-of-the-art results on all four benchmarks. The table reports the average success rate and the relative gain over second place. These results show that the generalization and scaling gains of Xiaomi-Robotics-1 carry over to standard simulation evaluation.BenchmarkXR-1ours2nd BestRel. GainRoboCasa74.572.6+2.6%RoboCasa36557.446.6+23.2%VLABench59.153.2+11.1%RoboDojo13.938.80+58.3%Simulation evaluation. All benchmarks report average success rate (%). XR-1 = Xiaomi-Robotics-1; Rel. Gain = (XR-1 − 2nd best) / 2nd best. Higher is better.ConclusionXiaomi-Robotics-1 demonstrates a practical path for scaling robot foundation models: large-scale embodiment-free UMI pre-training breaks the robot data bottleneck, while real-robot and instruction alignment transfer that general capability to physical robots. Results show that the model scales neatly with data volume and model size during pre-training, and that this scaling behavior translates directly to post-training, where a stronger pre-trained model yields better out-of-the-box real-robot performance in unseen environments. The resulting foundation model adapts to new tasks from minimal data and achieves state-of-the-art performance on four challenging simulation benchmarks that emphasize generalization.Finally, we present an uncut footage of luggage packing.Citation@article{guo2026xiaomi, title={Xiaomi-Robotics-1: Scaling Vision-Language-Action Models with over 100K Hours of Real-World Trajectories}, author={Guo, Jun and Jin, Piaopiao and Li, Jason and Li, Peiyan and Li, Yingyan and Liu, Futeng and Peng, Wanli, and Qin, Optimus and Su, Yifei and Sun, Nan and others}, journal={arXiv preprint arXiv:2607.15330}, year={2026} }

命中 3 个 AI/科技关键词。

正文翻译

user_7832 2026/07/20 16:04
我不知道为什么这里的一些评论如此悲观。看视频的时候我笑得合不拢嘴。你是说我们终于有了可以洗衣服的机器人了？ 而且模型至少和啤酒一样免费？

引用歌词/模因，我们曾经祈祷这样的时刻。 这就是机器人技术的用途。

I'm not sure why some comments here are so pessimistic. I was grinning ear to ear while seeing the video. You're telling me we've finally got robots that can do our laundry? And the model is at least as free as beer?

To quote the lyrics/meme, we used to pray for times like this. This is what robotics should be used for.

There tends to be a lot of low-key (and some not-so-low-key) anti-Chinese xenophobia on HN, so that's likely a big part of it too.

sph 2026/07/20 18:25
更像是亲美例外主义，任何在其之外（无论是欧盟还是中国）创造的产品的公告都会遭到质疑和怀疑。

除非某样东西来自日本，否则它就是完美的，任何批评都是因为你不欣赏其独特的文化遗产。

More like pro-US exceptionalism, and any announcement for products created outside of it (whether it's EU or China) is met with doubts and skepticism.

Unless something is from Japan, then it's perfect and any criticism is because you don't appreciate its unique cultural heritage.

kakacik 2026/07/20 19:12
这在美国占主导地位的论坛上有那么令人震惊吗？我不会期望不同的情况，即使人们很努力，也不是 100% 客观的。

话虽这么说，世界上超过 95% 的人生活在美国境外，而且最近（以橙色人的第二个任期达到顶峰，但早在很久以前就开始了）美国实际上已经疏远了所有其他国家，无论是否民主。当权力被充满琐碎和精神问题的不稳定的人牢牢掌握时，就会发生这种情况，而且还明显腐败。

因此，分裂加深，正如这里所见，正如 20 世纪是美国的世纪一样，21 世纪也将是中国的世纪。美国现在的一举一动只会将整个局势进一步推向这个方向。因此，人们为自己的阵营欢呼，而不是为某个更大的共同的结盟朋友群体欢呼。

Is that so shocking on dominantly US forum? I wouldn't expect different situation, people are not 100% objective even if they try hard.

That being said, >95% of the world lives outside US and in recent times (culminating with orange man's second term but started way before) US has alienated literally every single other country, democratic or not. This is what happens when reins are firmly held by unstable people full of pettiness and mental issues, plus visibly corrupt too.

So division deepens as seen also here, and as much as 20th century was a century of US, 21st century is and will be one of China. Every move US makes now just pushes whole situation further into that direction. So folks cheer for their own camp, not some larger common group of aligned friends.

h8hawk 2026/07/20 22:27
他们很可能是高度关注 AGI（以及当前大语言模型）风险的有效利他主义者，担心大语言模型会引发叛乱并导致人类灭绝，他们认为这是全球面临的最严峻挑战。
They are likely Effective Altruists highly attuned to AGI (With current LLM's) risks, fearing an LLM uprising and human extinction, which they regard as the world's most critical challenge.

the name stopped being relevant a long time ago when they went from rating charities to coming up with horror stories about ai. "ineffective egotism" would be better for what they are now.

baal80spam 2026/07/20 21:15
我注意到的情况比这还要严重——任何把当前美国描绘得很好的帖子都会立刻被无情地踩到底部（你可以看看我发的帖子作为例子），而任何稍微带点“美国不好”倾向的内容都能被顶到最上面。这现状真让人悲哀。
I've definitely noticed even more than that - any submissions painting the current USA in a good light are instantly and mercilessly buried (you can check my submissions for examples), while anything even remotely on the "USA bad" side gets to the top. It's really a sad state of affairs.
楼层
adjejmxbdjdn 2026/07/20 22:27
你或许会把这当成“美国真差劲”的评论，但让我们来看看你发的那个关于美国预期寿命创历史新高（2024年）的链接。

这听起来是件好事，也确实如此。但这并不是美国独有的故事。事实上，其他富裕国家不仅在新冠疫情前就已经拥有更高的基准预期寿命，而且在疫情后恢复到该水平的速度也比美国恢复到其原本较低水平的速度要快。

所以这实际上是一个“全球现代医学真棒”的故事，而遗憾的是，即便在这个大背景下，美国的表现也是最差的之一。

You may take this as “USA bad”, comment, but let’s take the U.S. life expectancy reaches record high (in 2024) link you posted.

That sounds like a good thing and it is. But it’s not a US story. In fact, other wealthy countries didn’t just already have a higher baseline life expectancy pre-COVID, they rebounded to it post-COVID sooner than the U.S. was able to its already lower level.

So what this actually is, is a story of “global modern medicine good”, and unfortunately, even within that, the U.S. is among the worst performing.

楼层
apropos_ 2026/07/20 21:30
我试着读了你分享的那篇《华盛顿邮报》专栏文章，讲的是对美国未来保持乐观的理由。结果我刚往下滑，内容就被爱国烟花动画和付费墙给替换了。这手法未免太刻意了。
I tried to read the Washington Post op-ed piece you shared on reasons to be optimistic about America’s future. As soon as I scrolled down, the content was replaced by a patriotic fireworks animation and a paywall. Seemed a bit on the nose.

thegrim33 2026/07/20 22:48
就像这个帖子——最高赞评论和整个评论区的大部分都被“谁敢对中国说哪怕一点负面评价，你们有什么毛病”这种言论给攻占了。
aka this very thread - top comment and most of the entire comment section has been taken over by literally "how dare anyone say anything even remotely negative about China, what's wrong with you".
楼层
yanhangyhy 2026/07/21 14:13
我认为这是因为媒体。它从来没有讲述真实的中国，相反它充满了假新闻和刻板印象。所以中国以外的人无法获得真实的信息。

我有一个很好的例子：稀土探测。我在 reddit/x 上看到了很多关于这个主题的文章，很多时候，它都附有一张老农民/老兄在肮脏的环境中干活的照片。所以这意味着这只是一项肮脏的工作，西方国家不想这样做，因为它很脏而且他们不想损害环境。只有中国不关心这个，

例如maple链接： 1. https://www.channelnewsasia.com/world/us-china-rare-earths-d... 2. https://www.thenationalnews.com/business/economy/2026/07/16/...

但实际上，事实并非如此。这是很久以前的事实，假设中国没有先进的技术，这完全是疯狂的，考虑到中国已经加入了这个行业并在技术上处于领先地位。但在大多数评论区，人们仍然认为这是肮脏的，所以只有中国在这样做。

如果你不了解你的“敌人”，你就会一直输。这种情况发生过很多次。

i think it's because the media. it never tells the real china, instead it's full of fake news and sterotypes. so people outside of china cannot get the real information.

i have one good example: the rare earth probelm. i saw many artices on this topic in reddit/x, and many times, it comes with a pic of an old farmer/dude in a dirty environment doing it's work. so it implies this is just a dirty work, western countires dont want to do it is because it's dirty and they dont want to harm the environment. only china dont care about this,

exmaple link: 1. https://www.channelnewsasia.com/world/us-china-rare-earths-d... 2. https://www.thenationalnews.com/business/economy/2026/07/16/...

but in reality, its not true. its only true long time before, assume china didn't have advanced tech on this is totally insane, consider china has joined this indsutry for decaseds and lead in the tech. but in most comments area, people still assume it's dirty so only china is doing it.

if you dont know your 'enermy', you will keep lose. This happens so many times.

楼层
Gareth321 2026/07/20 21:03
这很奇怪，因为我对 HN 的印象是亲中国的评论。我想这取决于主题、一天中的时间以及每个政府在特定日期使用多少个机器人来处理关键词及其计算预算。这个提交对我来说相当平衡。
It's strange because my impression has been pro-Chinese comments on HN. I suppose it depends on the topic, the time of day, and how many bots each government is employing on a given day for the key words and their compute budget. This submission seams rather balanced to me.
楼层
bob778 2026/07/20 21:54
到底有没有证据表明存在国家支持的机器人账号？这似乎已经成了一个公认的事实，但我没看到任何数据能证明它确实存在。
Is there evidence of any state-sponsored bots at all? It seems like this has become an accepted fact but I don’t see any data indicating it’s actually real.
楼层
versteegen 2026/07/20 22:37
我很确定没有。

HN 最近有很多被隐形封禁的机器人账号（我开启了 showdead 功能 [1]），但我从没注意到有带政治倾向的。不过我想，如果民族国家在 Hacker News 上搞活动，肯定会狡猾得多，不会让机器人账号那么明显

[1] 因为很多帖子被误判，显然部分原因是有些用户手太欠，只要看到和 AI 生成的文本有偶然相似之处就举报（大家，求求你们别再这么干了），部分原因是 HN 对新用户的自动审核非常严格，还有一部分原因是很多人被不公平地隐形封禁，或者尽管他们还在写很多有价值的帖子却依然被封。我甚至还救过一个人，结果发现他是被版主不小心误封的。

Pretty sure there isn't.

HN has lots of hellbanned bots lately (I have showdead on [1]) but I never notice ones pushing a political slant. But I suppose nation states conducting a campaign on Hacker News would be much too cunning to make the bots obvious

[1] because many posts get wrongly flagged, apparently in part by trigger-happy users who will flag anything with incidental similarities to AI prose (everyone, PLEASE STOP DOING THAT), in part because HN's automoderation is severe for new users, and in part because plenty of people get hellbanned either unfairly or although they do still write plenty of worthwhile posts. I even rescued someone who it turned out had been hellbanned by a mod by accident.

楼层
shevy-java 2026/07/20 18:29
好吧。我很久以前就已经决定我不想拥有我的 只要橙王还活着，钱就会流入美国公司 他的种族灭绝幻想（即使没有这一点，我已经对苹果、微软、Facebook和谷歌如此公然和暴力地虐待其他所有人感到恼火不已，包括购买立法——看看在你可以再访问万维网信息之前的强制性年龄嗅探运动；这是法西斯主义，儿童被虐待作为这里改变的虚假理由）。但我对中国成为 在整个产品链中也占据主导地位。我认为生产 全球范围内应该更加平衡。而且，非常如此， 本地。我也知道，由于各种原因，这是很难做到的， 但如果我有一点影响力的话我真的宁愿不 赋予美国或中国比他们已经虐待其他所有人更多的权力 在这里。顺便说一下，两者都是如此。
Well. I already decided some time ago that I don't want to have my money go into US corporations as long as the orange King is living out his genocidal fantasies (and even without that, I am already annoyed to no ends about Apple, Microsoft, Facebook and Google abusing everyone else so blatantly and violently, including buying legislation - look at the mandatory age sniffing movement before you can access information on the www any longer; this is fascism, and children are abused as fake reason for change here). But I am not all that happy about China becoming so dominant in the whole product chain either. I think the production should be more balanced world-wide, globally. And also, very much so, locally. I am also aware that this is very hard to do, for various reasons, but still if I have a little influence then I really would rather not empower the USA or China any more than they already abuse everyone else here. And both do, by the way.
